reviews.review_msg: This man is bad at every thing he does. He was bad enough as a prof. He was chronically late and felt unenthusiastic and uninvolved. He's doing worse as the math coordinator, as I heard that he made most if not all lower division math course midterms on the same day and didn't change anything despite the numerous complaints and requests.

Taking this class with Adam is do-able, but you have to put A LOT of effort in. His lectures were fine, but since he comes across as not caring about the material at all, it is hard to care yourself. He is chronically late to lectures too.

I can not speak for the people that took upper level math courses. He had easily understandable notes. He puts a variety of questions on the exams, which I love because most professors focus on a certain chapters and If you dont understand that chapter but understand everything else, the mark wont reflect your knowledge on the course...
god awful "professor" if you can even call him that. avoid for the love of your gpa.

Honestly I don't think Adam was that bad. I think MATH155 is just very difficult in general. He was always late which was annoying to some but honestly I didn't really mind. I HIGHLY suggest you go to the Math workshop at least once a week and get help with your IQs. It helps so much. Also, the key to doing well in this class is tons of practise
Just not great lecture slides

He's a masters student, so it's to be expected that he isn't as well seasoned as other senior lecturers. However, being behind on material covered due to being late 5-10 minutes and then skimming material that I think most students would consider important was a big red flag. His first midterm was awful but he decreased the difficulty afterwards
Tough marker, hard to reach, and non linear note taking
Unreasonable and can't teach, not sure why he's still here
Just very uninvolved
unreasonable

Adam: instead of marshalling his powers of concentration, limited as they may be, to become a good calc 2 teacher, adam decided to take a turn and screw us all over by combining 2 and 3 at the same time. Furthermore, he was 5-10 minutes late every single lecture. I strongly recommend that you avoid him for the love of your GPA.

Adam was terrible at teaching and his exams did not reflect my understanding of the course very well. do not take this prof for math 155 unless you want to put alot of effort and receive a C+ at best. Would not recommend at all.

I genuinely cannot understand those who gave adam a four or even a five. He is absolutely horrid because he is uninterested in the concept of the course nor does he care about the students' learning even the tiniest bit. adam is incompetent at what he does, yet he shows a lack of time and effort it needs to excel as an instructor.

His first midterm was pretty rough, but he took the constructive feedback and made the second midterm and final much easier. He was super accessible for extra help and really cares about his students. Sadly, late to class a lot. Overall I would still recommend this prof :)

Great professor! He posts clear filled notes after lectures. The midterms and assignments were fair and based on course material. The final exam was great to demonstrate your knowledge. Study his notes and read the textbook and youre set. Hes also very good with replying to emails and explaining concepts that you dont understand. Thank you Mr. D

Exams are long but if you actually study off the review packages then you should be fine, the actual questions themselves arent that hard but you dont really have time to pause during exam. his notes are straightforward and easy to grasp. his class definitely requires a lot of work but its easy if you put the time into it

Apart from the fact that this prof is quite monotone (which makes it hard to stay focused in class, especially at 8:30am) he explains the concepts clearly. His exams are fair, although more on the difficult side, but as long as you keep up with the material throughout the semester it'll be possible to do well in this course.

Okay so he's not quite as bad as his reviews say, but he's a mediocre instructor at best. Honestly I just think he's inexperienced. His notes are pretty clear and helpful for studying, but lectures are extremely boring. His midterms are stupid long. You'll probably do alright if you just review the notes on your own and do practice problems.

Despite what everyone is saying on here, Adam is actually a great prof. He does heaps of examples and his notes are fantastic. Exams are long, so be ready for that but if you know what's up you should be fine. I know he seems a bit subdued, but that's just his personality guys! If you actually talked to him you'd realise he's pretty great.
Very helpful outside class. Exams are long but fair, you just have to know your stuff. Study hard, do lots of problems. It's a math class.

Homework takes at least 10 hours a week, cannot teach to save his life, AND rushes through classes and examples because hes behind on material since hes 5-10mins late almost every single lecture. Hes monotone, uninterested in the course material or his students. He doesn't reply to emails, and lied about the midterm averages and is a masters stdnt

Goes through examples too quickly and does not explain his work well. Doesn't know how to explain thoroughly. Class average was super low for the midterms. You need to put in A LOT of time and effort for this class but that does not equate to a good grade. Don't recommend this class with this prof.

Don't listen to the good ratings bc i'm pretty sure the only good ratings he gets are the ones that he gives to himself. never ever under any conditions take this prof (unless you are a math genius). Terrible terrible teaching! Everybody hates him
got a B-. he posted notes online. few hard questions on homework and examinations. only thing i disliked was that we had to pay for 'mobius'

Very lazy and very monotone. He will assign you homework based on stuff that weren't even taught in the course. Unwilling to adapt to students' needs and continues to be inefficient at teaching. Avoid him at all costs.

Took a financial hit and dropped his class without receiving any refund. Lost a decent amount of money, but this class deteriorated my mental health. Monotone, lazy, and not willing to help students succeed. After the midterm average was a fail, he continued to blame the students and refused to change anything for the next one.

It's a first year math. he's mono tone, breathes rlly loud, speeds through examples. you should be able to understand if you took 154. if not just google the little things that you dont understand. 2 assignments a week and you have to print out/fill out notes in class. notes are stright from the textbook they give you on canvas
I wish there was an option to give a rating of 0/5 because this prof doesn't even deserve that 1/5

He was by far the least punctual 'professor' I have seen so far. Sped through absolutely fundamental concepts of calculus because he would waste up to ten minutes of a fifty minute lecture every other day. He spent the first ten minutes of midterm class making announcements when he could have sent out emails.

If I had known what this profs tests were like, I would have dropped the class at the beginning of the semester. Time was wasted before the 1st midterm to do an announcement, which could have easily been an email. The material on the midterm was not the same concepts which were heavily focused on in class, and the marking scheme was unfair.

Constantly late to class. (5-10) minutes each and every lecture. There is a good reason why his math 100 and math 155 midterm averages are 50 or below. In lecture speeds through crucial topics too quickly. His one strong suit is one on one during his office hours he can explain well. Not in front of 400 people. Would recommend avoiding at all costs
He posted his lecture notes completed online. I got a B- in math 232. For contrast, I had a C- in calc 1.

Goes over course notes way too quickly. All he does in his lectures is reading the notes out loud in a very monotone voice. I withdrew from his class and I highly suggest that you also withdraw if you care about your GPA.

Goes too waaaay too fast to keep up with during lectures / is very monotone. Towards the end more than half the class stopped going which he was very salty about. At least his notes are good enough to teach yourself from (what I did). The questions on the midterms weren't that hard but there were way too many of them. Final exam was fair.
Doesn't teach any of the questions that are on the exam.

didnt care about student learning, his main focus was just to get through the material he had to. rarely replied to emails, especially if they were concerning grades. his class examples were taken from the textbook but he explained them in a much more confusing way. he is only a masters student and has a long way to go if he wants to become a prof.

He speeds through examples in class faster than you can take notes, and drones on in a monotone reminiscent of Professor Binns from Harry Potter. 60% of the class stopped coming to lectures, which made him so salty. Prepare to read a million textbook chapters since you won't learn much in class. The midterms were too long, but the final wasn't bad.

I did not enjoy his class at all. The course had a lot of material to learn. Adam was unenthusiastic during his lectures and I found it very had to pay attention. Even if I did manage to pay attention, it would be very hard to follow him as he writes extremely fast and does not explain concepts well. In addition, he made the tests long and hard.

this was his first time teaching this class. lectures kind of boring, but he did give exam tips during class. was pretty fast paced and he uploaded the notes to be filled in too late(day of). midterms were too long but final exam was fine. would recommend going to TAs or office hours as it helps a lot. textbook helps too.

Adam may know his stuff but he doesn't know HOW to teach. He uploads outline notes before the lecture and fills it in during class. As he fills it in, he reads it aloud in a monotone. That's his idea of teaching! After the first few classes, less that half the class showed up. Use Khan academy to learn if you take it with him and you'll do fine.

Adam is fine as a prof. He knows his stuff and can clearly explain concepts. The problem really is that the course has alot of material. Only negative thing in regards to Adam is that he has a bit of a monotonous voice (kinda like listening to an audio textbook). His midterms are hard in the sense that theres a huge time crunch.

Although midterm 2 was quite the disaster, the final itself (worth 50%) was not too bad, and this is from a person who literally studied for it on the morning of. Do the HW, visit the TAs if you have any questions or don't understand a concept, and honestly you should do fine. Though grades haven't come out yet, the final this sem was fair.

There is a huge difference between the notes from the lecture and the actual works. He did the simplest examples and the test questions are way harder. Homework questions are always posted later than told. Many errors with grades.

Pros: Great review classes. Drops hints in lecture for tests. Posts good notes online. Cons: Consistently late to class. (2-5 mins) Sometimes posts notes late. Lectures can be pretty dry. A couple times the test included stuff not taught in lecture. He's a decent teacher and generally fair in his tests. Would take again.
He is a very hard working professor. The exams are long but not hard. He asks usual and questions in midterm and final. He is one of the best profs in the mathematics department at SFU.

This prof was not good at all, he was consistently late for class and would blame it on the bus. He also moved through material really fast and, like others have already said, would give simple examples in lecture and have much more complicated homework questions. Overall, not a good prof

Adam's an adequate prof. Midterm #1 was incredibly easy; the 2nd requires much more understanding. Although there is a notable difference in the simplicity of the lecture material and that on the exam(s), hw and quizzes are very doable. Quizzes are derived straight from the hw questions and TAs are readily available. Plan for much self-studying.

His lectures, although monotone, are ok, but his midterms are rather hideous. They are just very long and I just cannot see anyone having enough time to look over his/her answers. The averages of the exams reflect this pretty well. You must have a good math foundation to survive his class.

Adam is so diosrganized, rolls into class 10 mins late almost every class. Does not help prep you for an exam whatsoever. Does easy examples in class and posts practice exams the day before the exam. First midterm average was normal. Second midterm was 35% and he said he wont be curving it? Makes sense. Overall would not reccomend.

Adam is a okay prof, first midterm was nice and easy and the second one was brutal. He is organized and is accessible out of class. The in class quizzes are easy as they are straight of the textbook. He explains concepts clearly. The level of difficulty between in class and hw is somewhat unfair and he needs to provide more practice exams.

Adam is not a good professor. His lectures are just him doing easy example problems. He is not good at explaining what he is doing or why and talks in a voice so monotone it's reminiscent of getting served by miserable minimum wage worker at your local Mc D's. He is also sarcastic and doesn't really appear to care how his students do in the class.

He is always late, doesn't know his own syllabus, doesn't know why there are grading mistakes or changes in assignment dates. Very bad explainer and when you ask for clarification he goes back and explains it the same way...

Adam gives you way too much work that doesn't even help come quiz and midterm time. Explains the most basic examples during lecture and then has way harder questions on the quizzes and midterms. I would only recommend this course if you need it to get into another man one. Thank god I studied precal before doing this class with this guy

He is usually late for every class even though I see him walking in the hallways near the lecture hall... He moves through concepts very quickly and does not take time to elaborate on anything. Weekly Quizzes are also his flaw as he uses easy examples in lecture and switches it up to hard questions on the quiz. Does not seem to care about teaching

He is late for almost every class. His lectures are extremely dry, and he does not really explain the concepts clearly. His examples are too easy and the questions on quizzes are much harder so you are not prepared for the test questions. He also has the most monotone voices and sounds like he hates teaching, which makes it hard to learn in class

Adam may move quickly through concepts as mentioned in other reviews, but that is to be expected in a university course. He asks lots of questions to encourage participation and group learning and it's not his fault when he is left with no response. He is very clear about what is expected and seems to care about the success of his students.

This prof is new which explains his faults but he really needs to slow down during lectures. Basically you have to learn how to write crazy fast and listen or you'll be teaching yourself the material at home. The notes don't contain many examples which makes the homework even harder. I do not recommend taking him for linear 232

Adam has his own lecture notes, which covers only the basic parts and left the hard parts outside and expect us to learn from the book by ourselves. Quiz was given every week and 2 questions but the material is insane on how he combined 3 chapters into 1
